notes: No 8-K quarterly earnings files found in filings directory — current stock price not available from local files
All dollar amounts extracted from HTML text of 10-K filings; amounts in thousands (000s) per filing header
2020, 2022, and 2023 were net loss years; 2021, 2024, and 2025 were net income years

2024 net income of $1,862.1M is anomalously high vs operating income of $179.8M — driven by a $1,574.5M non-cash deferred tax benefit (income tax valuation allowance release), a one-time non-recurring accounting item
Pinterest has two share classes: Class A (one vote/share) and Class B (ten votes/share)
Gross profit is not a separate line in Pinterest's income statement; calculated as Revenue minus Cost of Revenue

data.currentPriceSource: Not available — no 8-K earnings files present in local filings directory
data.sharesSource: 10K-2025.htm, Balance Sheet — Class A: 585,458,698 + Class B: 79,679,925 = 665,138,623 total shares as of December 31, 2025
data.industry: AI-powered visual search and discovery platform / Digital advertising — from 10K-2025.htm, Item 1

rows: 10K-2020.htm, Consolidated Statements of Operations; net loss per share basic and diluted = -$0.22; diluted = basic because antidilutive in loss years
10K-2021.htm, Consolidated Statements of Operations — diluted EPS $0.46; diluted shares 691,651K
10K-2022.htm, Consolidated Statements of Operations — net loss per share basic and diluted = -$0.14; diluted = basic in loss years
10K-2023.htm, Consolidated Statements of Operations — net loss per share basic and diluted = -$0.05; diluted = basic in loss years
10K-2024.htm, Consolidated Statements of Operations — diluted EPS $2.67 (includes $1,574.5M one-time non-cash deferred tax benefit)
10K-2025.htm, Consolidated Statements of Operations — diluted EPS $0.61; diluted shares 687,771K
eps.notes: 2024 EPS spike driven by non-recurring $1,574.5M deferred tax asset recognition (valuation allowance release), not operating improvement. In loss years (2020, 2022, 2023), diluted shares = basic shares because including dilutive securities would be antidilutive. Underlying operating EPS trend is more modest. YoY growth comparisons between profit and loss years are not meaningful and marked N/A.

rows: 10K-2020.htm, Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows — Operating: $28,826K; CapEx (Purchases of property and equipment and intangible assets): $17,401K
10K-2023.htm, Cash Flows — Operating: $612,961K; CapEx: $8,063K. FCF also confirmed in 10K-2025.htm free cash flow reconciliation table
10K-2024.htm, Cash Flows — Operating: $964,594K; CapEx: $24,606K. Confirmed in 10K-2025.htm free cash flow reconciliation table
10K-2025.htm, Cash Flows — Operating: $1,284,264K; CapEx: $32,375K. Confirmed in free cash flow reconciliation table ($1,251,889K FCF)
fcf.calculation: FCF = Net cash provided by operating activities - Purchases of property and equipment (CapEx). Note: Pinterest is asset-light (AWS cloud infrastructure) so CapEx is very low relative to revenue. Does not include capitalized software development costs.

rows: 10K-2020.htm — Net Loss: -$128,323K; Equity: $2,242,371K (10K-2021.htm balance sheet prior-year column confirms)
roe.notes: ROE formula: Net Income / Total Stockholders' Equity. Pinterest has positive equity in all years. 2024 ROE inflated by $1,574.5M one-time non-cash deferred tax benefit. Underlying 2024 operating income was only $179.8M.

rows: 10K-2020.htm, Balance Sheet — no long-term debt line items found
10K-2021.htm, Balance Sheet — no long-term debt found
10K-2022.htm, Balance Sheet — no long-term debt found
10K-2023.htm, Balance Sheet — no long-term debt found
10K-2024.htm, Balance Sheet — no long-term debt found
10K-2025.htm, Balance Sheet — no long-term debt found
debt.notes: Pinterest has a revolving credit facility (mentioned in 10K-2025.htm Risk Factors and Notes) but no drawn balance — the company carries zero long-term debt on its balance sheet across all 6 years. This is a significant financial strength indicator for a Buffett-style analysis.

capex.calculation: CapEx % = (CapEx / Net Income) × 100. Pinterest is asset-light — CapEx is extremely low (< $33M even at $4.2B revenue) because infrastructure is AWS-based. In profit years, CapEx % is well below 25%, which Buffett considers favorable. N/M = Not Meaningful in loss years.

rows: 10K-2021.htm — Revenue: $2,578,027K; COR: $529,320K; Op Income: $326,187K; Net Income: $316,438K
10K-2023.htm — Revenue: $3,055,071K; COR: $688,760K; Op Loss: -$125,678K; Net Loss: -$35,610K (note: includes $126.9M restructuring charge)
10K-2024.htm — Revenue: $3,646,166K; COR: $750,355K; Op Income: $179,817K; Net Income: $1,862,106K (includes $1,574.5M one-time tax benefit)
10K-2025.htm — Revenue: $4,221,767K; COR: $841,521K; Op Income: $319,883K; Net Income: $416,855K
margins.calculation: Gross Profit = Revenue - Cost of Revenue (Pinterest does not present a separate Gross Profit line; calculated from stated components). Gross Margin = Gross Profit / Revenue. Operating Margin = Operating Income / Revenue. Net Margin = Net Income / Revenue.

rows: 10K-2020.htm, Consolidated Statements of Operations Data — Revenue: $1,692,658K
10K-2025.htm — Revenue: $4,221,767K (16% as reported, 15% constant currency per filing)

moat.text: Pinterest is an AI-powered visual search and discovery platform positioned at the intersection of search, social, and commerce. Platform accessible via mobile app and web. Unique value proposition: 'go from inspiration to action all on one consumer internet property.'
Demographic moat: approximately two-thirds of users are women; over 50% of user base is Gen Z. Platform resonates strongly with high-intent shoppers at the top of the purchase funnel.

Global scale: present in over 100 countries, 619 million monthly active users (MAU) as of year-end 2025. MAU growth: 459M (2020) → 431M (2021, COVID normalization dip per 10K-2021) → 450M (2022) → 498M (2023) → 553M (2024) → 619M (2025). After 2021 post-COVID normalization dip, resumed sustained growth.
moat.source: 10K-2025.htm, MD&A; MAU history cross-referenced across all 10-K filings

moat.text: Network effects: each additional pin, board, and user interaction improves content recommendations and visual search quality, creating a compounding content asset. Users create billions of Pins that serve as durable inventory for future discovery sessions.

Commerce integration moat: Product Pins, shopping spotlights, and direct checkout capabilities create a unique end-to-end purchase journey from discovery to transaction. This differentiates Pinterest from pure social or pure search platforms.
Zero long-term debt across all 6 years (2020-2025), $0 drawn on revolving credit facility. Strong balance sheet provides competitive resilience and financial flexibility.

management.text: CEO: William Ready — Principal Executive Officer. Named throughout risk factors as key person. Ben Silbermann (co-founder) is non-executive Chair of the Board.
management.source: 10K-2025.htm, Risk Factors and Executive Officers sections

management.text: Capital allocation: No long-term debt; operating cash flows growing strongly (from $28.8M in 2020 to $1.284B in 2025). FCF has grown from $11.4M (2020) to $1.252B (2025), demonstrating disciplined capital allocation and scalability of the business model.
management.source: 10K-2025.htm and prior 10-K filings, Cash Flow Statements

management.text: Share count management: Total diluted shares roughly stable 2021-2025 (691.7M → 687.8M), suggesting management has managed dilution from stock-based compensation through buybacks or controlled issuance. Note: SBC is very high relative to net income (e.g., $880M SBC vs $417M net income in 2025).

Path to profitability: First consistently profitable on an operating basis in 2024 ($179.8M op income) and 2025 ($319.9M op income). Prior to 2024, heavy investment years with operating losses despite strong gross margins and FCF generation.
management.source: 10K-2024.htm and 10K-2025.htm, MD&A overviews; cross-referenced with prior 10-Ks

industry.text: Key risk: User retention and engagement — 'If we fail to add new users or retain or recover users, or if users engage less with us, our business, revenue and financial results could be harmed.'

Advertiser dependency risk: Revenue is nearly 100% dependent on advertising. 'If we are unable to provide our advertisers with the ability to effectively target their advertising campaigns... we may not be able to attract new advertisers or retain existing ones.'

Data privacy regulatory risk: 'If we are unable to collect, process and use data because of data privacy laws, regulations, and legal decisions, it could impact our ability to effectively deliver relevant content.' Increasing global data privacy legislation (GDPR, CCPA, etc.) is a material risk.

Cloud infrastructure concentration risk: 'We depend on Amazon Web Services for the vast majority of our compute, storage, data transfer and other services. Any disruption of, degradation in or interference with our use of Amazon Web Services could negatively affect our operations.'

International growth opportunity and risk: 'If we are unable to execute our strategy on international growth and manage the complexity of global operations successfully, our business, revenue and financial results could be harmed.' International users are majority of MAU base but generate lower ARPU than US users.

Credit facility covenant risk: Company has a revolving credit facility. 'If we fail to comply with the covenants under the revolving credit facility, lenders would have a right to terminate the commitments to provide additional loans.' No balance drawn as of all filing dates.

consumer.text: Monthly Active Users (MAU): 459M (2020) → 431M (2021, COVID normalization dip) → 450M (2022) → 498M (2023) → 553M (2024) → 619M (2025). 5-year CAGR (2020-2025) of approximately 6.2%. Resumed strong growth after 2021 normalization.

User demographics indicate high commercial intent: two-thirds female user base; over 50% Gen Z users. Pinterest users 'come to Pinterest with high commercial intent to find ideas and inspiration' — described as being at the top of the purchase funnel, which commands premium advertising rates.

Revenue per user (ARPU) trend: ARPU growing as advertiser tools improve and commerce integration deepens. Revenue grew 149% from $1.693B (2020) to $4.222B (2025) while MAUs grew only 35% in same period, implying significant ARPU expansion and improving monetization efficiency.
consumer.source: Calculated from 10K-2020.htm and 10K-2025.htm, Revenue and MAU disclosures

consumer.text: Platform stickiness indicators: Pinterest users engage with 'Pins' (saved ideas organized into Boards) — creating a personalized content graph that increases switching costs. Content is curated over time, making the platform increasingly valuable to returning users.

AI-powered personalization: Pinterest described as 'AI-powered visual search and discovery platform' — suggesting ongoing investment in recommendation quality to drive engagement and reduce churn. Visual search (Lens feature) creates a unique discovery pathway not available on competing platforms.
